title: S0/G6: Where is the 'connective' hypothesis used? (boundedness-below)

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body: **Hypothesis-tracking gap (F2/faithfulness).** The theorem is stated for a *connective* $G$-spectrum. The solution must (a) use connectivity explicitly — it is needed for $\Phi^H$-detection of connectivity and for convergence of the isotropy-separation induction in G4 — and (b) not over-claim the characterization for non-connective $X$. State precisely the boundedness-below hypotheses at each step where they are invoked, and give a remark/counterexample showing the connective hypothesis cannot simply be dropped.
